peptides.name: Copper Peptide (GHK-Cu)
peptides.molecular_target: Upregulates collagen, elastin, and decorin synthesis; modulates metalloproteinases.
peptides.clinical_use: Accelerates wound healing, skin rejuvenation, and exhibits anti-inflammatory properties.
peptides.reported_side_effects: Generally none topically; potential flushing or localized irritation if concentration is excessively high.

wiki_content.summary: A naturally occurring copper complex of the tripeptide glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine. It has high affinity for copper(II) and was first isolated from human plasma.
wiki_content.mechanism: Activates copper-dependent enzymes and modulates gene expression related to tissue repair. It promotes collagen and elastin synthesis while acting as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent.
effects: Accelerates dermal and bone wound healing.
Stimulates angiogenesis and nerve outgrowth.
Improves skin elasticity and reduces fine lines.

peptides.name: Argireline (Acetyl Hexapeptide-8)
peptides.molecular_target: Mimics the N-terminal end of the SNAP-25 protein; competitively inhibits the SNARE complex formation to destabilize vesicle release of acetylcholine.
peptides.clinical_use: Topical reduction of expression wrinkle depth caused by repetitive muscle movement.
peptides.reported_side_effects: Exceedingly low toxicity profile; mild skin dryness or peeling in sensitive individuals if overused.

peptides.name: BPC-157
peptides.description: No clinical description available via PubChem.
peptides.molecular_target: Upregulation of Growth Factor signaling (VEGFR2) and collagen synthesis pathways.
peptides.clinical_use: Body Protection Compound-157 is widely researched for its regenerative properties in tendon, muscle, and ligament healing, as well as its protective effects on the gastric mucosa.
peptides.reported_side_effects: Few reported side effects in research settings; long-term human safety profiles are still under investigation.

wiki_content.summary: A pentadecapeptide derived from human gastric juice, recognized for its exceptional cytoprotective and regenerative capabilities.
wiki_content.mechanism: Upregulates VEGFR2 expression and stimulates the formation of new blood vessels (angiogenesis). It also promotes the outgrowth of tendon fibroblasts and increases collagen deposition at injury sites.
effects: Heals ligament, tendon, and muscle tears.
Protects the gut lining and reverses NSAID-induced damage.
Neuroprotective effects on brain and peripheral nerves.

peptides.name: Epitalon
peptides.description: Epitalon is an organic molecular entity.
peptides.molecular_target: Telomerase enzyme activation and Pineal gland chromatin regulation.
peptides.clinical_use: A synthetic tetrapeptide known for its ability to activate telomerase and regulate melatonin production. It is studied for its anti-aging and life-extension potential.
peptides.reported_side_effects: No significant adverse effects reported in primary clinical trials; continues to be evaluated for long-term safety.

wiki_content.summary: A synthetic tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) based on a natural peptide (epithalamin) produced in the pineal gland.
wiki_content.mechanism: Acts as a telomerase activator, elongating telomeres in somatic cells. It regulates melatonin secretion and restores pineal gland sensitivity to hormonal signals.
effects: Extends cellular lifespan and reduces age-related pathologies.
Normalizes circadian rhythms and sleep patterns.
Demonstrates potent anti-tumor and antioxidant activity.

peptides.name: MOTS-c
peptides.description: No clinical description available via PubChem.
peptides.molecular_target: Mitochondria-nuclear signaling and AMPK-dependent metabolic activation.
peptides.clinical_use: Mitochondria-derived peptide that regulates metabolic homeostasis, insulin sensitivity, and exercise capacity. It acts as a signaling molecule for mitochondrial-nuclear communication.
peptides.reported_side_effects: Data on human side effects is limited; primarily investigated in preclinical metabolic models.

wiki_content.summary: A 16-amino-acid peptide encoded by the mitochondrial 12S rRNA gene, acting as a metabolic regulator.
wiki_content.mechanism: Translocates to the nucleus during metabolic stress and regulates nuclear gene expression. It activates the AMPK pathway and improves mitochondrial function.
effects: Enhances insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism.
Increases exercise capacity and physical performance.
Promotes healthy aging and longevity.

peptides.name: Semax
peptides.description: No clinical description available via PubChem.
peptides.molecular_target: Melanocortin receptors (MC4R) and BDNF signaling modulation.
peptides.clinical_use: Nootropic peptide used for cognitive enhancement, neuroprotection, and recovery from ischemic stroke. It modulates the expression of Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor (BDNF).
peptides.reported_side_effects: Generally well-tolerated. Rare instances of mild irritation of the nasal mucosa (if administered via spray).

wiki_content.summary: A synthetic heptapeptide analog of the ACTH (4-10) fragment, developed for its profound neurotrophic and nootropic properties without hormonal activity.
wiki_content.mechanism: Triggers rapid up-regulation of Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor (BDNF) and its high-affinity receptor, TrkB, within the hippocampus. Enhances long-term potentiation ($LTP$) and stabilizes neurotrophin mRNA during hypoxia.
effects: Pronounced increase in working memory and cognitive endurance.
Neuroprotective buffer against ischemic stroke and metabolic stress.

peptides.name: Selank
peptides.molecular_target: Modulates GABAergic neurotransmission and BDNF expression.
peptides.clinical_use: Designed for its potent anxiolytic and cognitive-enhancing effects. Used for generalized anxiety disorder and to improve focus.
peptides.reported_side_effects: Well-tolerated; very low incidence of side effects.

wiki_content.summary: A synthetic heptapeptide analog of the immunomodulatory peptide tuftsin, designed for its potent anxiolytic and cognitive-enhancing effects.
wiki_content.mechanism: Modulates GABAergic neurotransmission and influences the expression of Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor (BDNF). It also affects the metabolism of serotonin and norepinephrine.
effects: Reduces anxiety and emotional tension without sedation.
Enhances memory consolidation and focus.
Exhibits immunomodulatory properties during stress.

peptides.name: Ipamorelin
peptides.description: No clinical description available via PubChem.
peptides.molecular_target: Selective Ghrelin receptor (GHS-R1a) agonist.
peptides.clinical_use: Selective growth hormone secretagogue and ghrelin receptor agonist. Used to stimulate GH release without significantly affecting cortisol or prolactin levels.
peptides.reported_side_effects: May include flushing, headache, or slight water retention at high dosages.

wiki_content.summary: A selective growth hormone secretagogue and ghrelin receptor agonist.
wiki_content.mechanism: Mimics ghrelin and binds to the ghrelin receptor (GHS-R1a) in the pituitary gland, stimulating the release of growth hormone.
effects: Increases lean muscle mass and strength.
Promotes fat loss and improved metabolism.
Enhances recovery and sleep quality.
